canonical_question: How do I design a multi-tenant SaaS architecture?
aliases: shared database vs database per tenant
constraints: Tenant isolation is a security requirement, not optional -- a single leaked tenant_id in a query can expose all tenant data
Row-Level Security (RLS) policies must be enabled AND enforced -- enabling RLS without creating policies silently blocks all rows
Schema-per-tenant hits PostgreSQL catalog limits at ~5,000-10,000 schemas -- plan migration path before that threshold
JWT tenant claims must be validated server-side -- never trust client-supplied tenant_id from headers or query params alone
Database connection pooling (PgBouncer) in transaction mode breaks SET app.current_tenant -- use session mode or pass tenant context per query

skip_this_unit_if.condition: Building a single-tenant self-hosted application with no shared infrastructure
Need to isolate tenants at the infrastructure level (separate VPCs, accounts, clusters)
skip_this_unit_if.use_instead: AWS Control Tower / Azure Landing Zones for account-per-tenant isolation

skip_this_unit_if.condition: Designing a marketplace or platform with user-generated content (not SaaS tenancy)

inputs_needed.question: What level of tenant isolation do you need?
options: Hybrid (pool for small, silo for enterprise)

inputs_needed.question: How many tenants do you expect at scale?
Do tenants require data residency or compliance isolation (SOC 2, HIPAA, GDPR)?
